2026-07-05.title: Venezuela Housing Warnings Went Unheeded Before Deadly Quakes
2026-07-05.facts: Residents, construction experts, and seismologists had been sounding alarms for years about Venezuela's public housing—and now their worst fears have come to pass. The buildings that collapsed in last week's earthquake had been flagged as vulnerable to natural disasters long before the ground started shaking.

2026-07-05.title: Philippines Moves to Impeach Vice President Sara Duterte
2026-07-05.facts: The impeachment trial of Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte is set to begin, a dramatic escalation in the country's political crisis. Duterte, who has said she plans to run for president, faces allegations of corruption and charges that she made death threats against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.

2026-07-05.title: Trump Administration Rolls Back Dozens of Gun Regulations
2026-07-05.facts: The Trump administration has dismantled dozens of federal gun regulations, marking one of the most significant rollbacks of firearm oversight in recent memory. The changes reverse rules put in place during the Biden presidency, essentially returning gun regulations to where they stood before 2021.

2026-07-05.title: Typhoon Bavi Bears Down on U.S. Pacific Territories
2026-07-05.facts: Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands are bracing for a direct hit from Typhoon Bavi, a massive tropical cyclone churning through the Pacific with the force of a Category 5 hurricane. The storm is forecast to pass near the U.S.

2026-07-05.title: Yosemite Overwhelmed After Trump Administration Ends Reservation System
2026-07-05.facts: Yosemite National Park is drowning in its own popularity this summer, with visitors enduring exceptionally long waits after the Trump administration eliminated a reservation system that had controlled access during peak season. The decision has turned one of America's most iconic parks into a bottleneck of traffic jams, packed trails, and overwhelmed facilities.

2026-07-05.title: June Jobs Report Signals Bond Market Opportunity
2026-07-05.facts: June's jobs report is weaker than the headline numbers suggest, and bond investors are taking notice. The latest employment data shows enough softness to make fixed-income markets look increasingly attractive, even as stock investors focus on other metrics.

2026-07-05.title: Federal Rule Shifts Roof Replacement Costs to Homeowners
2026-07-05.facts: A new federal insurance rule is forcing homeowners to shoulder more of the cost when it's time to replace a roof—and the timing couldn't be worse with hail and hurricane season underway. The regulation has allowed insurers to shift a bigger portion of roof-replacement expenses onto policyholders, leaving many facing a tough choice when storms hit.

2026-07-05.title: Ukraine's Drone Campaign Triggers Russia's Worst Fuel Crisis in Decades
2026-07-05.facts: Ukraine is striking Russian energy infrastructure at an unprecedented pace, and it's starting to show. Kyiv's intensified drone campaign has hit so many refineries, pipelines, and storage facilities that Russia is now facing its worst fuel crisis in decades.

2026-07-05.title: Trump Account Investment Rules Unveiled
2026-07-05.facts: Parents who received money in so-called "Trump accounts" for their children now know exactly where they can invest it. The Treasury Department just released guidance specifying that the funds must be placed in low-cost index funds, and they've published a list of eligible options families can choose from.

2026-07-05.title: The Cromans Return to Manhattan Multifamily
2026-07-05.facts: Jake and Adam Croman are quietly rebuilding the family name in New York real estate—and they're doing it by betting on exactly the kind of buildings most investors won't touch. The brothers have been assembling a portfolio of small Manhattan apartment buildings, focusing heavily on rent-stabilized assets that the broader market has avoided since the 2019 rent law overhaul made value-add plays far less profitable.

2026-07-05.title: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift Tie the Knot at Madison Square Garden
2026-07-05.facts: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift got married Friday night at Madison Square Garden in what's being described as one of the year's most star-studded ceremonies. The Kansas City Chiefs tight end and the pop superstar said their vows in front of a celebrity-packed guest list at the iconic New York venue.

2026-07-05.title: 76ers Land Jaylen Brown, Make Push for LeBron
2026-07-05.facts: The 76ers pulled off one of the biggest moves of the offseason by acquiring Jaylen Brown in a trade—and they're not done yet. Just one day after landing Brown, Philadelphia has entered the hunt for LeBron James and is actively trying to convince him to join the team, according to league sources.

2026-07-05.title: Lakers Ship Deandre Ayton to Washington in Surprise Deal
2026-07-05.facts: The Lakers are sending center Deandre Ayton to the Washington Wizards in exchange for guard Jaden Hardy and two future second-round picks. It's a move that clears significant salary for Los Angeles while giving Washington some much-needed size in the middle.

2026-07-05.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7
2026-07-05.facts: Anthropic just dropped Claude Opus 4.7, the latest iteration of its flagship reasoning model—and the performance benchmarks are making waves. The new release pushes improvements in what Anthropic calls "extended thinking," letting the model spend more compute on harder problems before delivering an answer.

2026-07-06.title: Venezuela Earthquake Response Reveals New U.S. Aid Strategy
2026-07-06.facts: The Trump administration's approach to the Venezuela earthquake crisis looks nothing like the massive relief operation the U.S. mounted for Haiti after its 2010 disaster.

2026-07-06.title: French Court to Rule on Marine Le Pen's Presidential Eligibility
2026-07-06.facts: Marine Le Pen's political future hangs in the balance as a French appeals court prepares to deliver its verdict on whether she can run for president in 2027. The leader of France's National Rally party is currently appealing a conviction that could bar her from holding public office.

2026-07-06.title: China Fires Submarine Missile Into Pacific, Rattling Neighbors
2026-07-06.facts: China test-fired a long-range ballistic missile from a submarine into the Pacific Ocean, sending a clear signal of its growing military reach just as Australia locked down new defense agreements with Pacific Island nations. The launch marks a rare public demonstration of China's submarine-launched missile capabilities, a key component of its nuclear deterrent.

2026-07-06.title: Iran Mourns Khamenei as Calls for Retaliation Intensify
2026-07-06.facts: Enormous crowds packed the streets of Tehran for a third straight day as Iran held funeral processions for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with mourners calling for revenge against the United States and Israel. The scale of the gatherings reflects both the leader's decades-long grip on power and the state's ability to mobilize massive public displays of grief and anger.

2026-07-06.title: Research Universities Slash Ph.D. Programs Amid Federal Funding Uncertainty
2026-07-06.facts: Some of America's top research universities are shrinking their doctoral programs, and it's a troubling sign for the future of American science. The cuts are being driven by uncertainty over federal funding, which has traditionally been the backbone of graduate research support.

2026-07-06.title: Trump Appoints Former Defense Attorney to Federal Bench
2026-07-06.facts: Emil Bove defended the president in court, and now he's getting a lifetime appointment as a federal judge. President Trump announced he's nominating Bove to the federal bench, continuing a pattern of appointing his former personal lawyers to judgeships.

2026-07-06.title: Prosecutors Detail Murder Case in Charlie Kirk Killing
2026-07-06.facts: A preliminary hearing is underway today in the murder case that shocked the country—the killing of Charlie Kirk. Prosecutors will lay out their evidence against the accused killer as a judge decides whether there's enough to proceed to trial.

2026-07-06.title: Samsung Eyes Major Price Hike as Chip Shortage Tightens
2026-07-06.facts: Samsung Electronics is reportedly planning to raise prices on its memory chips by 20%, a move that signals just how tight the global semiconductor market has become. The proposed increase would hit dynamic random access memory chips—the kind that go into everything from smartphones to data centers.

2026-07-06.title: Oil's Retreat From Danger Zone Could Boost Stocks
2026-07-06.facts: Oil prices have pulled back sharply from their recent highs, and Wall Street analysts say that's good news for the broader economy. Evercore ISI points to historical patterns suggesting that when oil exits what they call the "danger zone," stocks tend to rally.

2026-07-06.facts: EasyJet says it's reached an agreement in principle on a £5.2 billion takeover by U.S. investment firm Castlelake, but the market isn't exactly celebrating.

2026-07-06.title: Manhattan's Ultra-Luxury Condo Market Hits Decade High
2026-07-06.facts: Manhattan's new development market just posted its strongest quarter in years—but only if you're shopping above $10 million. Contracts signed for new condos asking $10 million or more nearly doubled in the second quarter compared to last year, jumping from 21 to 38 deals, according to Brown Harris Stevens Development Marketing.

2026-07-06.title: Florida Closes Live Local Loophole That Protected Cities From Lawsuits
2026-07-06.facts: Florida just made it harder for cities to push back against affordable housing developments—and buried the change deep in a bill that Governor Ron DeSantis recently signed. House Bill 1389 expands the definition of a "person" under the state's fair housing law to include agencies, government entities, and other legal or commercial entities, a shift specifically aimed at discouraging local governments from challenging Live Local Act applications.

2026-07-06.title: England Survives Thriller to Knock Mexico Out of World Cup
2026-07-06.facts: England's scraped through to the World Cup quarterfinals with a nerve-shredding 3-2 victory over Mexico in the round of 16. The match was anything but smooth—England found themselves navigating a rollercoaster that included three major VAR incidents, according to a breakdown of the controversial calls.

2026-07-06.title: Lakers Ship Ayton to Washington in Center Shakeup
2026-07-06.facts: The Lakers are sending center Deandre Ayton to the Wizards in exchange for guard Jaden Hardy and two future second-round picks. It's a significant move for Los Angeles as they continue reshaping their roster heading into next season.

2026-07-06.title: Patrick Ewing Joins Wizards Coaching Staff
2026-07-06.facts: Patrick Ewing is heading back to the NBA bench. The Georgetown and Knicks legend has agreed to join the Wizards as an assistant coach under head coach Brian Keefe.

2026-07-06.title: Claude Code Hooks Let You Build Guardrails for Autonomous Agents
2026-07-06.facts: You're already running scheduled tasks with Claude Code, but here's the pattern most people miss: the four hooks—PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, and SessionStart—give you precise interception points to build guardrails around autonomous runs without touching the main prompt. PreToolUse fires right before Claude calls a tool, so you can log the intent, check parameters against a whitelist, or block high-risk actions like file deletions or API calls to production endpoints.

2026-07-07.title: Messi and the Battle for Maradona's Legacy
2026-07-07.facts: Lionel Messi's dominating the World Cup, but off the pitch there's a multimillion-dollar war being fought over Diego Maradona's image. 2026-07-07.title: China Launches Nuclear Missile Over the Pacific

2026-07-07.title: Supreme Court Takes Up AR-15 Ban Cases
2026-07-07.facts: The Supreme Court has agreed to hear challenges to two state assault weapons bans, and the decision could reshape what firearms the government is allowed to restrict. The justices are expected to focus on whether AR-15s and similar rifles are in "common use" — a legal standard that could determine if they're protected by the Second Amendment.

2026-07-07.title: Sexual Assault Accuser Details Allegations Against Maine Senate Candidate
2026-07-07.facts: A woman has gone public with detailed allegations that Graham Platner, the Democratic nominee for Senate in Maine, raped her years ago. 2026-07-07.facts: Samsung Electronics just posted record quarterly earnings, and shareholders responded by dumping the stock. The South Korean tech giant delivered numbers that would normally have investors celebrating, but the market proved to be a tough crowd.

2026-07-07.title: Brooklyn Heights Townhouse Logs Borough's Biggest Sale of the Year
2026-07-07.facts: A Civil War-era townhouse on Brooklyn Heights' waterfront just changed hands for $24.5 million, making it the borough's priciest residential deal so far this year. 2026-07-07.title: City Council Freezes Interest on Delinquent Property Taxes
2026-07-07.facts: New York City's newly adopted budget includes a significant change for property owners who've fallen behind on their taxes: the City Council has frozen interest charges on delinquent property tax bills. 2026-07-07.title: LeBron James Leaves Lakers in Stunning Split
2026-07-07.facts: LeBron James is walking away from the Los Angeles Lakers, ending one of the most scrutinized partnerships in NBA history after what he announced Monday would be his final game in purple and gold. 2026-07-08.facts: Marine Le Pen wasted no time launching her French presidential campaign—she announced it within hours of a court upholding her guilty verdict. An appeals court confirmed the conviction this week, but the ruling hasn't derailed the nationalist leader's political ambitions.

2026-07-08.facts: A Boeing cargo plane bound for Karachi has vanished over the Arabian Sea after reporting a navigation system fault. The aircraft lost contact with air traffic control shortly after the crew alerted controllers to the malfunction, triggering an urgent search operation.

2026-07-08.facts: Dharmesh Patel won't face trial for driving his family off a California cliff after prosecutors dropped three counts of attempted murder following his completion of a court-ordered mental health program. The stunning reversal comes after Patel drove his wife and two children over a cliff in what authorities initially called a deliberate act.

2026-07-08.title: America's Trade Gap Hits Widest Mark in Over a Year
2026-07-08.facts: The U.S. trade deficit swelled in May to its highest level in more than a year, driven by a record surge in imports.

2026-07-08.title: Fed Chair Warsh May Ditch Forward Guidance, Sparking Volatility Fears
2026-07-08.facts: Kevin Warsh could upend decades of Federal Reserve playbook by making policy decisions without telegraphing them to markets first. That's the warning from James Bullard, a former president of the St.

2026-07-08.title: Australia's Biggest Telecom Suffers Massive Outage
2026-07-08.facts: Servers at Australia's largest telecommunications company crashed, knocking out train systems and emergency call services across the country. The outage hit data centers in both Sydney and Melbourne, crippling critical infrastructure that millions depend on.

2026-07-08.title: Wall Street Bull Tells Investors to Buy as Markets Enter Bear Territory
2026-07-08.facts: The stock market's tumbled into bear territory, but one prominent Wall Street strategist says this is exactly the time to start buying. Tom Lee of Fundstrat is urging investors to buy the dip, shrugging off losses that have rattled markets in recent weeks.

2026-07-08.title: South Korea's Stock Market Plunges Into Bear Territory
2026-07-08.facts: What was the world's hottest stock market just months ago has now officially entered bear territory. South Korea's benchmark index has fallen twenty percent from its record high in June, marking a dramatic reversal for what had been a darling of global investors.

2026-07-08.title: Developer Pushes Back on Pfizer Building Collapse Fears
2026-07-08.facts: The developer converting the old Pfizer headquarters in Midtown East says reports of an imminent collapse are overblown, even as city officials ordered an emergency evacuation Tuesday after discovering buckled support beams. Nathan Berman of MetroLoft told reporters the situation's been "blown a little bit out of proportion," despite dramatic videos showing sagging upper floors and warped columns at the massive office-to-residential conversion project.

2026-07-08.title: Messi Makes History as Argentina Survives Egypt Scare
2026-07-08.facts: Lionel Messi scored in a record sixth straight World Cup knockout match, leading Argentina to a stunning 3-2 comeback win over Egypt in Atlanta. Argentina was down late before Messi delivered yet again, dragging his team into the quarterfinals with what observers are calling another virtuoso performance.

2026-07-08.title: Former Cowboys Defender Kneeland Diagnosed with CTE

2026-07-08.title: Donovan Mitchell Signs $273 Million Extension With Cavaliers
2026-07-08.facts: Donovan Mitchell just locked in one of the richest deals in NBA history. The Cavaliers guard has agreed to a four-year, $273 million maximum extension that keeps him in Cleveland through the end of the decade.

2026-07-08.title: Claude Cowork Goes Mobile as Anthropic Shifts from Desktop to Your Pocket

2026-07-09.title: Mexico Probes Alleged U.S. Deception in Drug Lord Capture

2026-07-09.title: Le Pen Kicks Off Presidential Bid After Court Clears Her to Run
2026-07-09.facts: Marine Le Pen launched her presidential campaign in rural France just one day after a court ruled she could run for office again. The far-right leader's campaign kickoff drew both cheers and boos in a region that's long been a stronghold for her National Rally party.

2026-07-09.title: India Blocks Release of Film on Punjab Police Brutality
2026-07-09.facts: Indian censors have demanded 127 cuts to a film exploring a dark chapter of police violence in Punjab, effectively blocking its release for years. The film, titled "Satluj" and featuring popular artist Diljit Dosanjh, examines a period of police brutality in the northern state that remains a sensitive topic.

2026-07-09.title: U.S. and Iran Trade Second Night of Strikes as Ceasefire Collapses
2026-07-09.facts: The United States and Iran have exchanged attacks for a second consecutive night, all but ending an interim ceasefire that had brought a brief pause to hostilities. U.S.

2026-07-09.title: Second Federal Task Force Shooting Death in Memphis This Week
2026-07-09.facts: Memphis is dealing with its second fatal shooting involving a federal task force in less than a week. Tennessee authorities are now investigating both incidents, which have left community members demanding answers about the use of force by federal agents operating in the city.

2026-07-09.title: Global Growth Forecast Dims as Commodity Prices Bite
2026-07-09.facts: The International Monetary Fund just cut its global growth forecast for this year, and high commodity prices are taking most of the blame. The IMF now projects world economic output will expand at just 3 percent in 2026, down from its earlier estimate.

2026-07-09.title: Drugmaker Loses $27 Billion as Heart Disease Trial Fails
2026-07-09.facts: A major pharmaceutical company saw $27 billion in market value evaporate Thursday after announcing its experimental heart disease drug crashed in a Phase III clinical trial. Two companies had been jointly developing the treatment, and both confirmed the trial failed to meet its primary endpoints.

2026-07-09.title: SK Hynix Offering Could Be Second-Largest Equity Deal Ever
2026-07-09.facts: SK Hynix is about to make Wall Street history. The South Korean chipmaker's Nasdaq listing, set to price Friday, could become the second-largest equity offering ever recorded, trailing only SpaceX's blockbuster deal.

2026-07-09.title: Anthropic Locks Down Entire Hudson Square Tower in AI Office Expansion
2026-07-09.facts: Anthropic has leased an entire 16-story building in Hudson Square, cementing its place in Manhattan's AI office boom. The company's taking over all 466,000 square feet at 330 Hudson Street from AEW Capital Management, a massive jump from its current 15,000-square-foot footprint at 155 Sixth Avenue that it signed just two years ago.

2026-07-09.title: Verlander to Hang Up His Cleats After This Season
2026-07-09.facts: Justin Verlander is calling it a career. The 43-year-old pitcher announced he'll retire after the 2026 season ends, bringing down the curtain on one of baseball's most decorated careers.

2026-07-09.title: Bucks Trade Giannis Antetokounmpo in Franchise-Altering Move
2026-07-09.facts: Milwaukee just made one of the most stunning decisions in recent NBA history. The Bucks have traded Giannis Antetokounmpo, and general manager Jon Horst isn't sugarcoating it—he called the move a "very difficult decision." The two-time MVP has been the face of the franchise for years, leading Milwaukee to its first championship in nearly five decades back in 2021.

2026-07-09.title: Donovan Mitchell Signs Extension to Stay in Cleveland
2026-07-09.facts: Donovan Mitchell is staying put. The Cavs' star guard has agreed to a contract extension with Cleveland, ending months of speculation about whether he'd test free agency or push for a trade.

2026-07-09.title: China Claims Security Flaws in Claude Code—Anthropic Pushes Back
2026-07-09.facts: China's cybersecurity authority just issued a public warning that Anthropic's Claude Code contains what it's calling "backdoor" vulnerabilities that could let data leak to foreign servers. The alert, posted by China's National Computer Virus Emergency Response Center, specifically flags the autonomous coding tool as a risk and advises Chinese users to avoid it.

2026-07-10.title: Venezuela's Youth Baseball League Devastated by Twin Earthquakes
2026-07-10.facts: Venezuela's youth baseball league—a symbol of national pride for decades—is counting its dead after last month's twin earthquakes. Children who played in the league have been left injured, orphaned, or killed in the disaster that's now claimed at least 920 lives.

2026-07-10.title: China Lands Reusable Rocket for the First Time
2026-07-10.facts: China's pulled off its first successful landing of a reusable rocket, according to state media—a milestone that puts the country in the same club as SpaceX and Blue Origin. The landing marks a major step forward for China's space ambitions, which have accelerated dramatically over the past decade.

2026-07-10.title: Trump Administration Guts Election Commission With Mass Firings
2026-07-10.facts: The Trump administration has fired multiple members of the Election Assistance Commission, rendering the independent body effectively useless just months before the midterm elections. The commission, which sets voting standards and certifies equipment used across the country, also saw one resignation in the shake-up.

2026-07-10.title: ICE Shooting Victim Was Father and Business Owner With Decades in U.S.
2026-07-10.facts: Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, the man shot by Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents, was a father, husband, and business owner who'd lived in the United States for 35 years after moving from Mexico. The shooting has drawn intense scrutiny over ICE's use of force during enforcement operations.

2026-07-10.title: National Guard Shooting Suspect Hospitalized After Refusing Food
2026-07-10.facts: The man charged with murdering a National Guard member is now hospitalized in what a judge called "dire" condition after refusing to eat. The suspect has pleaded not guilty to murder and other charges stemming from the shooting.

2026-07-10.title: Delta Absorbs Record Fuel Costs But Still Beats Profit Expectations
2026-07-10.facts: Delta Air Lines just reported something that sounds impossible: record-high quarterly fuel expenses and a profit beat at the same time. The carrier absorbed the highest fuel bill in its history this past quarter, yet still managed to top Wall Street's earnings expectations and post record revenue.

2026-07-10.title: Personal Bankruptcy Filings Surge Nearly 50 Percent in Three Years
2026-07-10.facts: Americans filed for personal bankruptcy at a staggering rate last year—roughly 1,500 filings every single day. That's a 47 percent jump from 2022 to 2025, marking one of the sharpest increases in financial distress in recent memory.

2026-07-10.title: SK Hynix Raises $26.5 Billion in Largest Foreign Debut on Nasdaq
2026-07-10.facts: South Korean chip giant SK Hynix just pulled off the largest U.S. stock market debut by a foreign company in history, raising $26.5 billion in a mega share sale.

2026-07-10.title: Earnings Season Kicks Off With Wild Stock Swings
2026-07-10.facts: Second-quarter earnings season is off to a rocky start, and even companies that beat expectations are getting punished by the market. Stocks are seeing increasingly wild swings in the days after they report results, a sign that investors' sky-high expectations are tough to meet.

2026-07-10.title: Airbnb Buys Infamous "Anna Delvey" Building in Manhattan
2026-07-10.facts: Airbnb's just closed on one of Manhattan's most notorious addresses. The short-term rental giant teamed up with Boston's Beacon Capital Partners to buy 281 Park Avenue South—the ornate, Beaux Arts landmark that fake heiress Anna Delvey tried and failed to turn into a private club.

2026-07-10.title: NFL Players Face Four Times Higher Risk of Dementia Death
2026-07-10.facts: NFL players are four times more likely to die from neurodegenerative diseases like dementia and Parkinson's than the general population, according to a study released Wednesday. It's the latest data point in what's become an unavoidable reckoning for professional football: the long-term cost of playing the game.

2026-07-10.title: Kawhi Leonard Trade on Ice Amid Salary Cap Investigation
2026-07-10.facts: The Clippers and Raptors struck a deal to send Kawhi Leonard north of the border, but don't expect him in a Toronto uniform anytime soon. The NBA's put the entire trade on hold while it investigates whether Los Angeles circumvented the salary cap.

2026-07-10.title: Claude's Extended Thinking—And How to Burn That Budget Wisely
2026-07-10.facts: Anthropic's discovered something wild inside Claude: a hidden intermediate space where the model essentially thinks out loud before answering you. They're calling it extended thinking, and it's now exposed in a way you can control and budget.

2026-07-11.title: Cuba Plunges Into Darkness Again
2026-07-11.facts: Cuba's gone dark—again. The island suffered its second complete nationwide blackout in just five days, leaving millions without power across the entire country.

2026-07-11.title: Spanish Wildfire Death Toll Climbs to Twelve
2026-07-11.facts: Spain is battling one of its deadliest wildfires in years, and the toll keeps rising. At least twelve people are confirmed dead, including four believed to be British nationals, with another twenty-three still missing as flames tear through the countryside.

2026-07-11.title: China's Factory Workers Face Robot Replacement
2026-07-11.facts: In Kunshan, a Chinese manufacturing hub that helped build the country's electronics boom, laid-off factory workers now spend their days in the park—because there's nowhere else to go. They've been replaced by robots as China accelerates its drive toward automation, and the human cost is becoming impossible to ignore.

2026-07-11.title: Russian Missile Barrage Hits Kyiv
2026-07-11.facts: Russia fired a fresh wave of missiles and drones at Kyiv, wounding eleven people in one of the war's deadliest months for the Ukrainian capital. The latest attack involved ballistic missiles and a massive swarm of 121 drones, part of an intensified Russian campaign targeting civilian infrastructure.

2026-07-11.title: Representative Detained by Israeli Settlers in West Bank
2026-07-11.facts: Representative Ro Khanna spent ninety minutes blocked by Israeli settlers in the West Bank before he was allowed to leave. The California Democrat's visit wasn't about showing support for Israel—it was the opposite.

2026-07-11.title: Fed Chair Warsh Faces First Congressional Testimony
2026-07-11.facts: Kevin Warsh is about to get his first real grilling as Fed chair. He'll testify before Congress this week, and lawmakers are eager to pin him down on where he thinks the economy is headed.

2026-07-11.title: New Housing Law Offers Incentives for Local Governments
2026-07-11.facts: A new housing law just hit the books, and while it's not going to remake the American landscape overnight, it does put some real money on the table. The legislation offers local governments and builders financial incentives to make incremental changes—things like easing zoning restrictions, speeding up permitting, and encouraging denser development near transit.

2026-07-11.title: EasyJet Accepts Surprise Takeover Bid
2026-07-11.facts: EasyJet is about to change hands in a deal that caught the aviation industry off guard. The British budget airline has agreed to a takeover bid from Apollo, a US firm, which swooped in and trumped a recent offer from another suitor, Castlelake.

2026-07-11.title: Meta Stock Surges on AI Pricing Strategy
2026-07-11.facts: Meta just wrapped up its best week in years, and artificial intelligence is the reason why. The company's stock surged after it unveiled new low-cost pricing for its AI services and laid out ambitious infrastructure plans that have investors betting big on the social media giant's tech future.

2026-07-11.title: State Housing Official Exposes Secret Policy Shift That Blocked Building Owners
2026-07-11.facts: A former New York State housing official has gone public with allegations that regulators secretly changed policy in 2023 to make it nearly impossible for landlords to remove buildings from rent stabilization. Woody Pascal, who ran the state's substantial rehabilitation program from 2010 until recently, says he learned of the change at a mid-2023 meeting with other housing officials—no public discussion was held, and the real estate industry received zero notice.

2026-07-11.title: Hochul Distributes $1 Billion to Create and Preserve 2,754 Apartments Statewide
2026-07-11.facts: Governor Kathy Hochul's administration just awarded $1 billion in housing bonds and subsidies to a dozen development projects across New York, part of her broader $25 billion five-year housing plan. The funds, distributed by New York State Homes and Community Renewal, will create or preserve 2,754 apartments statewide.

2026-07-11.title: NFL Players Face Four Times Higher Dementia Risk
2026-07-11.facts: NFL players are dying from dementia and other neurodegenerative diseases at four times the rate of the general population, according to a study released Wednesday. The research found that former players face dramatically elevated risks of Parkinson's and similar conditions compared to people who never played professional football.

2026-07-11.title: Wembanyama Locks Down Historic Extension With Spurs
2026-07-11.facts: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs have agreed to a five-year, $252 million maximum rookie-scale contract extension, making it the third-largest rookie extension in NBA history. The deal keeps San Antonio's franchise cornerstone locked in through the prime years of his career.

2026-07-11.title: Anthropic Discovers Hidden "Thinking Space" Inside Claude's Neural Architecture
2026-07-11.facts: Anthropic just published research showing they've found a hidden layer inside Claude where the model appears to puzzle through concepts before answering—something they're calling a "global workspace." It's a breakthrough in understanding what actually happens inside large language models when they process a prompt. The research team observed that Claude maintains an internal space where it seems to work through ideas, almost like a scratch pad, before committing to an output.

2026-07-12.title: Taliban Tightens Grip on Afghanistan's Most Liberal City
2026-07-12.facts: Afghanistan's Taliban government has imposed full control over Herat, a city that once stood out for its cosmopolitan culture and looser social norms—even during previous Taliban rule. The crackdown marks a shift for a place that had been something of an outlier in the country's increasingly restrictive landscape.

2026-07-12.title: Typhoon Bavi Slams China After Deadly Path Across Asia
2026-07-12.facts: Typhoon Bavi made landfall in China's Zhejiang province, forcing the evacuation of nearly two million people and shutting down schools, businesses, and transport services across the region. The storm had already killed 17 people in the Philippines before skirting Japan and hitting Taiwan on its way north.

2026-07-12.title: Senator Lindsey Graham Dies at 71
2026-07-12.facts: Senator Lindsey Graham died Saturday evening after what his office called a "brief and sudden" illness, ending a more than two-decade career as one of the Senate's most prominent voices on foreign policy. The South Carolina Republican was 71.

2026-07-12.title: White House Directs FBI Investigation Into Times Reporting on Air Force One
2026-07-12.facts: FBI Director Kash Patel spent roughly eight hours at the White House on Friday overseeing an investigation tied to New York Times reporting about Air Force One security. The probe has already led to subpoenas being issued to several Times reporters who worked on the story.

2026-07-12.title: Maricopa County Election Dispute Could Shape National Midterms
2026-07-12.facts: A power struggle over who controls elections in Arizona's largest county is becoming a test case for the midterms nationwide. Maricopa County's Republican-dominated Board of Supervisors is locked in a bitter fight with a Trump-aligned official over election administration in Greater Phoenix.

2026-07-12.title: New Housing Law Offers Incentives to Expand Supply
2026-07-12.facts: A new housing bill just became law, and while it's not sweeping legislation, it does give local governments and builders some financial incentives to chip away at the country's housing shortage. The measure focuses on incremental changes rather than dramatic overhauls, encouraging communities to ease zoning restrictions and builders to add more units.

2026-07-12.title: Aldi Bets Nine Billion on American Expansion
2026-07-12.facts: German discount grocer Aldi is pouring nine billion dollars into a major U.S. expansion, and it's taking aim at urban markets like Manhattan with a pitch that's hard to ignore: four-dollar almond butter and prices that undercut nearly everyone.

2026-07-12.title: Douglas Elliman's AI Push Signals Major Shift for Real Estate Agents
2026-07-12.facts: Douglas Elliman just announced a major technology overhaul powered by Google Cloud AI—and it's raising uncomfortable questions about how many agents the industry really needs. CEO Michael Liebowitz said the tech upgrade will lead to layoffs at the firm, but he didn't stop there.

2026-07-12.title: Seahawks Sold for Record $9.6 Billion
2026-07-12.facts: The Seattle Seahawks are about to change hands in the most expensive sports franchise deal ever recorded. A group led by Vinod Khosla has agreed to purchase the team for $9.612 billion, smashing previous records for NFL franchise sales.

2026-07-12.title: White Sox Select Cholowsky First Overall in MLB Draft
2026-07-12.facts: The Chicago White Sox made UCLA shortstop Roch Cholowsky the number one pick in the 2026 MLB draft Saturday, continuing a rebuild that's been years in the making. Cholowsky becomes the latest college infielder to go first overall, a trend that's accelerated as teams prioritize polish and proximity to the majors.

2026-07-12.title: Wembanyama Locks In Record Extension with Spurs
2026-07-12.facts: Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs have agreed to a five-year, $252 million maximum rookie-scale contract extension, sources tell ESPN. It's the third-largest rookie extension in NBA history.

2026-07-12.title: Claude Reflection Dashboard Gives You X-Ray Vision Into Why Your Prompts Fail
2026-07-12.facts: Anthropic just launched the Claude Reflection Dashboard, and it's the debugging tool you didn't know you were missing. The dashboard shows you exactly what Claude was "thinking" when it gave you an answer—surfacing its internal reasoning steps, tool-use decisions, and the specific points where it got confused or made a wrong turn.

2026-07-13.title: Gunmen Kill Ecuadorean Drug Lord's Brother in Fake Police Raid
2026-07-13.facts: Men disguised as police officers murdered David Macías in Ecuador over the weekend, in what authorities are calling a targeted assassination. Macías was the brother of notorious drug lord José Adolfo Macías—known as "El Fito"—who's currently awaiting trial in the United States.

2026-07-13.title: Japan Building New Intelligence Agency With Western Support
2026-07-13.facts: Japan is breaking with decades of post-World War II security limits to build a new intelligence agency, and it's getting help from the West. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is driving the effort, citing mounting threats from both Russia and China as Japan rethinks its pacifist stance.

2026-07-13.title: Senator Lindsey Graham Dies
2026-07-13.facts: South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham has died. The sharp-witted Republican was known as one of the Senate's most mercurial figures—able to repair fractured relationships with political rivals and insert himself into nearly every major legislative battle during his long tenure.

2026-07-13.title: Five Killed in Illinois Family Shooting
2026-07-13.facts: A shooting in southern Illinois has left five members of one family dead and two others wounded. Illinois State Police announced Sunday that two teenage suspects are now in custody following the deadly incident.

2026-07-13.title: Supreme Court Seeks Bigger Security Budget
2026-07-13.facts: The Supreme Court is heading to Capitol Hill with an unusual request—and an unusual pair of messengers. Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan are scheduled to testify before Congress about the court's request for more than $200 million in additional security funding.

2026-07-13.title: Oil Spikes as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz
2026-07-13.facts: Oil prices surged as much as 5% on Monday after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to shipping. The move comes amid escalating attacks between the U.S.

2026-07-13.title: Taiwan's Chipmaker Posts Record Revenue Ahead of Earnings
2026-07-13.facts: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation just reported a record month for revenue, with June sales climbing 67% to $13.2 billion. It's a striking number that underscores how insatiable demand remains for the advanced chips TSMC produces.

2026-07-13.title: South Korean Chipmaker Plunges After U.S. Debut
2026-07-13.facts: SK Hynix saw its local shares in Seoul plummet 15% on Monday, just one day after a highly successful debut of its U.S. depositary receipts.

2026-07-13.title: Mortgage Rates Jump on Iran Tensions, But Homebuyers Keep Coming
2026-07-13.facts: Mortgage rates have climbed in response to the escalating conflict in Iran, yet housing demand is defying the spike. Pending home sales rose to nearly 64,000 last month, up from just over 61,000 in the same period last year.

2026-07-13.title: Record-Breaking Sale Sees 49ers Minority Owner Buy Seattle Seahawks
2026-07-13.facts: A group led by Niners limited partner Vinod Khosla has agreed to purchase the Seattle Seahawks for $9.612 billion, sources tell ESPN—making it the most expensive sports franchise sale in history. The price tag shatters previous records and underscores just how valuable NFL ownership has become.

2026-07-13.title: Warriors Add Vogel as Kerr's Top Assistant
2026-07-13.facts: The Warriors are bringing in Frank Vogel as their new associate head coach, filling the hole left by Terry Stotts' departure. Vogel will work directly under Steve Kerr, adding another experienced voice to Golden State's coaching staff.

2026-07-13.title: Claude Cowork's Real Sweet Spot: The Work You're Relieved to Offload
2026-07-13.facts: Anthropic's letting slip what Claude Cowork is actually being used for in the wild, and it's not the flashy autonomous projects you'd expect. The real action is in what they're calling "mundane office work nobody wants to own"—the spreadsheet reconciliations, the invoice chasing, the data entry that sits in everyone's queue until Friday afternoon panic sets in.

2026-07-14.title: Yemen's Houthi Strikes Spread to Saudi Arabia
2026-07-14.facts: Yemen's Houthi fighters launched missiles at Saudi Arabia, targeting Abha airport in retaliation for strikes on Sanaa's airport. The Iran-backed group blamed Saudi Arabia for the attack on Yemen's capital and struck back within hours.

2026-07-14.title: Japan Launches First Centralized Intelligence Agency Since WWII
2026-07-14.facts: Japan is building a centralized intelligence agency for the first time since World War II, a sweeping shift driven by threats from Russia and China. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is leading the effort with help from Western allies who are advising on how to structure the new organization.

2026-07-14.title: Supreme Court Justices Head to Capitol Seeking Security Funding
2026-07-14.facts: Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett are making a rare trip to Capitol Hill Wednesday to ask Congress for millions more in security funding. They'll be the first justices to testify before lawmakers since 2019, and the timing's no accident—threats against the court have escalated sharply in recent years.

2026-07-14.title: Immigration Agent Kills Man in Maine Vehicle Shooting
2026-07-14.facts: A federal immigration agent shot and killed a man during an encounter in Biddeford, Maine Monday morning. The victim has been identified as Joan Sebastian Guerrero, who died in a vehicle during the confrontation with ICE.

2026-07-14.title: Health Department Reverses $10 Billion Funding Freeze for Five States
2026-07-14.facts: The Department of Health and Human Services has backed down from a controversial decision to freeze $10 billion in funding for five Democratic-led states. The reversal came after the administration faced repeated legal setbacks in court challenges to the funding pause.

2026-07-14.title: JPMorgan Delivers Biggest Profit Beat in Five Years
2026-07-14.facts: JPMorgan just posted its largest profit beat in half a decade, powered by a surge in revenue from equity trading. The bank's earnings smashed Wall Street's expectations, driven by a record-breaking quarter for overall revenue.

2026-07-14.title: Bank of America Stock Drops Despite Earnings Beat
2026-07-14.facts: Bank of America's shares tumbled in pre-market trading even after the bank reported a fifteen percent jump in revenue compared to last year. The drop highlights a disconnect between strong financial performance and investor sentiment that's become increasingly common this earnings season.

2026-07-14.title: California Leads Lawsuit to Block Paramount Warner Bros Merger
2026-07-14.facts: California and eleven other states are moving to block the proposed merger between Paramount and Warner Bros, setting up a major legal fight over media consolidation. The lawsuit comes from states where both studios have significant operations, with California home to both companies' headquarters.

2026-07-14.title: City Hands NYPD Parking Lot to Land Trust for Affordable Housing
2026-07-14.facts: The Mamdani administration just awarded its first city-owned site to a community land trust, converting an NYPD parking lot in the East Village into 131 affordable apartments. The Cooper Square Committee, Housing Works, and Spatial Equity are partnering with This Land is Ours Community Land Trust to transform the police lot at 324 East 5th Street into housing, a senior center, and community space.

2026-07-14.title: Walker Wins Home Run Derby in Dramatic Finish
2026-07-14.facts: Jordan Walker just claimed the 2026 Home Run Derby title in spectacular fashion, homering on his final six swings to overtake hometown favorite Kyle Schwarber. The Cardinals slugger's late surge made him the first player from St.

2026-07-14.title: Lakers Acquire Walker Kessler in Trade
2026-07-14.facts: Walker Kessler is heading to the Los Angeles Lakers in a deal that cost the franchise both money and draft picks, giving the team a much-needed big man as it begins life without LeBron James. Kessler spoke to reporters after the trade and didn't mince words about his commitment.

2026-07-14.title: Claude Speaks Different Values in Different Languages
2026-07-14.facts: Anthropic just published research showing that Claude doesn't hold the same values when you talk to it in different languages—and the differences are bigger than you'd think. The company tested how Claude responds to ethical questions across languages like English, Spanish, Japanese, and Hindi, and found that the model's answers shift depending on which language you're using.

2026-07-15.title: India Expels Bangladeshi Immigrants, Sparking Border Crisis
2026-07-15.facts: India's pushing out thousands of Bangladeshi immigrants it says are in the country illegally, and Bangladesh isn't having it. The mass deportations have left people stranded along the border, caught between two countries that are increasingly at odds over who belongs where.

2026-07-15.title: Russian Strikes Hit Odesa as Infrastructure War Escalates
2026-07-15.facts: Russian bombs continue falling on Odesa, killing three people in the latest wave of attacks targeting Ukraine's critical port infrastructure. The strikes are part of an escalating pattern in recent days, with both Russia and Ukraine launching attacks on each other's facilities for shipping goods.

2026-07-15.title: China's Top Space Official Purged Over Corruption and Sex Charges
2026-07-15.facts: Beijing just expelled one of its most powerful officials from the Communist Party. Ma Xingrui, who once ran China's space program and later led the Xinjiang region, has been purged over corruption and sex-related charges.

2026-07-15.title: Bangkok Pub Fire Death Toll Climbs to 32
2026-07-15.facts: The death toll from a Bangkok pub fire has risen to 32, with 15 people still fighting for their lives in intensive care. Officials confirmed two more deaths on Tuesday, bringing the grim count higher as doctors work to save 30 others still hospitalized.

2026-07-15.title: Pleasure Boat Sinks Near Alcatraz, Killing One
2026-07-15.facts: A pleasure boat capsized in San Francisco Bay near Alcatraz on Tuesday, leaving one person dead and three others still missing. Sixteen passengers were pulled from the water and survived the accident, which happened in one of the bay's busiest tourist corridors.

2026-07-15.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Shifts Focus to the Strait of Hormuz
2026-07-15.facts: The military confrontation between the United States and Iran has entered a new and potentially more dangerous phase, with American forces now concentrating on the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump has resumed hostilities after the brief ceasefire collapsed, and the narrow waterway—through which a fifth of the world's oil passes—has become the focal point of operations.

2026-07-15.title: Inflation Cools to 3.5 Percent as Iran Conflict Pause Offered Brief Relief
2026-07-15.facts: Consumer prices climbed just 3.5 percent in June compared to a year earlier, marking the slowest pace of inflation in months. Even more striking: prices actually fell from May to June, posting their biggest monthly drop since 2020.

2026-07-15.title: New Fed Chair Warsh Pledges to Lower Inflation Without Revealing His Plan
2026-07-15.facts: Kevin Warsh made his debut before Congress as Federal Reserve chairman, promising to bring inflation down but offering virtually no detail on how he intends to do it. In his first testimony on Capitol Hill, Warsh reiterated the Fed's commitment to taming price pressures but didn't signal whether he supports raising interest rates further or taking a different approach.

2026-07-15.title: Thames Water Returns to Profit After Raising Customer Bills
2026-07-15.facts: Britain's troubled Thames Water has swung back to profitability after hiking what it charges households for water and sewage service. The utility, which serves millions of customers across London and southern England, reported a return to the black in its latest financial results.

2026-07-15.title: BlackRock Hits $15 Trillion Milestone as Profits Surge
2026-07-15.facts: BlackRock just crossed a threshold no asset manager has ever reached before: $15 trillion under management. The world's largest fund manager reported a jump in profits that sent its shares climbing, fueled by investors pouring money into both stocks and bonds.

2026-07-15.title: Bobby Cayre Unloads Meatpacking District Building for $100 Million
2026-07-15.facts: Bobby Cayre just closed the biggest commercial real estate deal to hit city records this week, selling a mixed-use property at 675 Hudson Street in the Meatpacking District for $100 million. The sale topped a busy day of transactions—177 deals totaling $612 million were filed in the 24 hours ending Tuesday afternoon.

2026-07-15.title: Zeckendorf Family Sues Supermarket Heirs Over Stolen Painting
2026-07-15.facts: The Zeckendorf family is accusing the owners of a Pennsylvania supermarket chain of selling a painting they say was stolen from them half a century ago. The lawsuit targets the heirs of Robert and Patricia Weis, who control nearly two-thirds of Weis Markets, over an abstract work by Pierre Soulages that sold at Christie's last fall for nearly $5 million.

2026-07-15.title: Spain Stun France to Reach World Cup Final
2026-07-15.facts: Spain are headed to the World Cup final after dismantling France 2-0 in Tuesday's semifinal, a result that's sent shockwaves through the tournament. France, one of the pre-tournament favorites, collapsed in spectacular fashion, failing to find any rhythm against a Spanish side that controlled the match from start to finish.

2026-07-15.title: Number One Draft Pick Cholowsky Gets Record-Breaking Bonus
2026-07-15.facts: The White Sox have agreed to terms with Roch Cholowsky, the number one overall pick in this year's MLB draft, and they've shattered the record for signing bonuses in the process. Cholowsky's deal reportedly includes a $10.35 million signing bonus, the largest ever handed to a drafted player.

2026-07-15.title: Timberwolves Acquire LaMelo Ball in Blockbuster Deal
2026-07-15.facts: The Timberwolves just made one of the biggest moves of the offseason, landing star point guard LaMelo Ball in what team president Tim Connelly is calling a franchise-altering trade. Connelly says he believes Ball can "elevate" the team's ceiling, signaling Minnesota's all-in on competing for a championship now.

2026-07-15.title: Claude Code Gets a Browser—and That Changes the Autonomous Agent Game
2026-07-15.facts: Anthropic just shipped a built-in browser for Claude Code, and the reason this matters isn't convenience—it's about breaking the Playwright ceiling. Until now, any site that blocked headless automation or used aggressive bot detection forced you to either abandon the task or hack together workarounds with Computer Use.
